#+title: Project Overview for [Project Name]

# Org Mode Syntax Reference
# Title - +title
# Heading 1 - * H1
# Heading 2 - ** H2
# Heading 3 - *** H3
# Heading 4 - **** H4
# Heading 5 - ***** H5
# Heading 6 - ****** H6
# Bullet list - - item
# Numbered list - 1. item
# Unchecked box - - [ ] item
# In-progress box - - [-] item
# Completed box - - [X] item
# Bold - *bold*
# Italics - /italics/
# Underline - _underline_
# Strikethrough - +strikethrough+
# Code - ~code~
# Verbatim - =verbatim=

* Document Relationships

AGENTS.org (barrel/overview)
├── docs/project/overview/requirements.org (vision, features, non-goals)
├── docs/project/overview/tech-stack.org (dependencies and tools)
├── docs/project/overview/design.org (system design, key decisions, trade-offs)
├── docs/project/overview/architecture.org (detailed implementation)
├── docs/project/overview/file-organization.org (folder structure)
├── docs/project/coding-guides/coding-laws.org (project coding standards)
└── docs/project/coding-guides/styling-guide.org (project styling standards)

* What is [Project Name]?
[Provide a brief description of the project, its purpose]

* Strict Project Guidelines
Before responding or contributing to the project, please ensure that you have reviewed the following guidelines:

- [ ] Review coding laws and regulations applicable to the project.
- [ ] Follow the coding standards and best practices outlined in the project documentation.
- [ ] Use consistent file organization and naming conventions as specified in the project guidelines.
- [ ] Ensure that your code is of high quality and maintainable, adhering to the project's coding standards.
- [ ] Document all changes and decisions made during the development process, including any new features, bug fixes, or architectural changes.
